brief_summary: Inline four-step process diagram for the H2 'The minimum that gets eighty percent of the benefit'. Four side-by-side panels connected by short arrows: (1) open switch / pull stem — top housing shown lifting off with arrows + lower body with exposed stem; (2) brush 205g0 on stem rails — isolated stem cross-section with amber dotted rail highlight + tapered brush approaching from upper-left; (3) lay film on lower-housing rim — lower housing cross-section with central well, amber dotted rim highlight, brush approaching; (4) spring + GPL 105 in baggie — baggie outline with two coiled springs and amber droplets. Each panel gets a numbered amber chip overhead and a sans label below. Matches the body protocol verbatim — no fabricated steps.
data_sources: apps/web/src/content/articles/lubing-101.mdx — the four-step protocol is described verbatim in the body paragraph: 'Open the switch. Pull the stem. Apply 205g0 with a small brush ... to the four stem rails ... Lay a film along the rails of the lower housing ... Drop the spring into a baggie with two or three drops of GPL 105.'
